stories.title: Cardinals 17, Cubs 1 — Game 1 Recap (July 3)
stories.angle: Peterson imploded (career-high 10 ER in 3.2 IP); Pallante dominant (5.2 IP, 1 R); Cardinals had 17 hits; Winn + Church 3-run HRs; Cubs' 5-game win streak snapped; historic Elias Bureau note — first team ever to win by 20+, then lose by 10+

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
Cardinals 17, Cubs 1. That one stung.

Peterson gave up 10 earned runs in 3 2/3 innings — a career high. Pallante was untouchable. St. Louis put up 17 hits and punched back hard.

Five-game win streak over. Bounce-back starts tonight.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

research.facts:
Cardinals 17, Cubs 1 — Game 1 Recap (July 3)
**Key Facts:**
- Final score: Cardinals 17, Cubs 1 — HIGH confidence (CBS Sports recap, ESPN box score, Viva El Birdos)
- Peterson (L): 10 ER allowed in 3 2/3 IP on 9 H — career-high 10 ER — HIGH confidence (CBS Sports/ESPN)
- Pallante (W, 10-5): 5 2/3 IP, 5 H, 1 BB — MEDIUM (single AI summary with inline context)
- Cardinals hits total: 17 — HIGH confidence (multiple sources)
- Winn: 3-run HR, 4 RBI — HIGH confidence (CBS Sports recap)
- Church: 3-run HR — HIGH confidence
- Burleson: 2 H, 4 RBI — HIGH confidence
- Bregman: 2 H, RBI double (7th inning) — HIGH confidence
- Game delay: 10 min (thunderstorms) — MEDIUM (single source)
- Cubs 5-game win streak ended — HIGH confidence (confirmed by record shift 49-38 → 49-39)
- Cubs record post-game: 49-39 — HIGH (series-context.json, generated July 4 morning)
- Cardinals record post-game: 46-39 — HIGH (series-context.json)
- Elias Bureau claim: "first team in MLB history to win by 20+ runs then lose next game by 10+ runs" — MEDIUM (one source; per Elias is credible but single-source; needs cross-reference)

stories.title: PCA Wins NL Player of the Month for June
stories.angle: .381 BA, 11 HR, 8 SB, 1.249 OPS in June; Ruth and Gehrig are the only other players to put up that combination of stats in a calendar month; 5.1 WAR, No. 3 in all of baseball; All-Star full roster announcement July 6

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Pete Crow-Armstrong wins NL Player of the Month for June.

.381 BA. 11 HRs. 8 SBs. 1.249 OPS. 5.1 WAR — No. 3 in all of baseball.

The only other players with a month like that? Babe Ruth and Lou Gehrig.

#Cubs #GoCubs #MLB

research.facts:
PCA NL Player of the Month, June
**Key Facts:**
- PCA named NL Player of Month for June — HIGH confidence (BCB, Bleacher Nation, BVM Sports, Yardbarker, Chicago Sun-Times, Yahoo Sports — all confirm same award)
- June stats: .381 BA, 11 HR, 5 2B, 2 3B, 20 RBI, 21 R, 8 SB, 1.249 OPS, 40 H in 105 AB — HIGH confidence (BCB, BVM Sports, multiple sources agree)
- Historical comp: Ruth/Gehrig comparison for .375+ BA, .775+ SLG, 80+ total bases, 15+ walks, 10+ HR, 5+ SB — HIGH confidence (Chicago Sun-Times by name; Yardbarker also)
- Season WAR: 5.1, No. 3 in baseball (behind Sánchez 5.8, Ohtani 5.4) — MEDIUM (one combined source; cross-reference Baseball Reference needed)
- All-Star reserve selection expected; full roster announcement July 6 — HIGH confidence (multiple sources)
**Sources:**
- Bleed Cubbie Blue: https://www.bleedcubbieblue.com/chicago-cubs-news/224120/pete-crow-armstrong-national-league-player-of-the-month-june
- Bleacher Nation
- Chicago Sun-Times: https://chicago.suntimes.com/cubs/2026/07/03/pete-crow-armstrong-had-a-june-that-compares-with-babe-ruth-and-lou-gehrig
- BVM Sports: https://bvmsports.com/2026/07/03/pete-crow-armstrong-wins-a-much-deserved-nl-player-of-the-month/
- Yardbarker

stories.title: The Whiplash Stat — Bounce-Back Framing
stories.angle: Elias Bureau stat about being first ever 20+/10+ swing team; 23-3 Padres sweep then 17-1 Cardinals loss; lean into the absurdity, set up the bounce-back narrative; Imanaga reset tonight

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
The Cubs just became the first team in MLB history to win by 20+ runs, then lose the very next game by 10+ runs.

23-3 sweep of the Padres. Then 17-1. That's a 24-hour whiplash for the ages.

Imanaga's on the mound tonight. Time to reset.

#Cubs #FlyTheW #GoCubs

research.facts:
Whiplash Stat / Bounce-Back
**Key Facts:**
- 23-3 Cubs win vs Padres (July 1) → 17-1 loss vs Cardinals (July 3) — HIGH confidence (confirmed in both July 2 and July 3 pipeline results)
- First team ever to win by 20+, then lose next game by 10+ (per Elias) — MEDIUM (single source, Elias is authoritative in baseball stats; but only one article cited it)
- Imanaga starts tonight — HIGH confidence (multiple preview sources)
**FACT-CHECK FLAG:** The Elias Bureau claim needs cross-reference. It will be noted in 06-fact-check-log.md.

stories.title: Trade Deadline — 30 Days, Rotation on Life Support
stories.angle: 30 days to August 3; Sonny Gray (9-1, 2.69 ERA) remains the target; Hoyer cannot wait longer

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
30 days to the trade deadline.

Steele is done for the year. Brown out a month-plus. Taillon rehabbing at Iowa. Horton done. Cabrera on IL.

Sonny Gray: 9-1, 2.69 ERA. Jed Hoyer, the call has to happen now.

#Cubs #MLB #GoCubs

research.facts:

- Trade deadline: August 3, 2026 — HIGH confidence (established throughout pipeline)
- 30 days away (from July 4) — HIGH confidence (math: Aug 3 - July 4 = 30 days)
- Sonny Gray record: 9-1, 2.69 ERA — HIGH confidence (multiple sources: Heavy.com, Yardbarker, CBS Sports, On Tap Sports Net all confirm same line)
- Gray has full no-trade clause — HIGH confidence (Heavy.com, multiple sources)
- Red Sox record: 34-46 (sellers) — MEDIUM (single source synthesis)
- Cubs injured starters: Steele (season-ending), Brown (month-plus), Taillon (Iowa rehab), Horton (season-ending), Cabrera (IL) — HIGH confidence for Steele/Brown/Taillon; confirmed across multiple dates in pipeline

stories.title: Wild Card Watch — Cubs 49-39, Cardinals 46-39, Tonight Matters
stories.angle: Cubs hold WC spot at 49-39; Cardinals surged to 46-39; tonight's Wrigley game is a de facto playoff position game

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Tonight's game at Wrigley has direct playoff implications.

Cubs: 49-39, holding a Wild Card spot.
Cardinals: 46-39, just 3 games back.

They won Game 1 17-1. This series isn't just a rivalry — it's a Wild Card battle.

#Cubs #NorthSiders #MLB

stories.title: Pre-Game — Imanaga at Wrigley on Independence Day
stories.angle: Imanaga is the stopper; Wrigley Field on July 4th is one of baseball's great settings; FOX national broadcast; bounce-back energy after yesterday's embarrassment

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
Shota Imanaga. Wrigley Field. July 4th.

The bounce-back game is here. Imanaga (5-6, 4.30 ERA) vs. Kyle Leahy and the Cardinals.

FOX. 7:08 PM CT. The Friendly Confines on Independence Day.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW
